summary: Jan-Dhan Gateway - Fraud-proof benefit distribution system with sequential validation engine, hash-linked ledger, three-key verification protocol (eligibility, budget, frequency gates), ID-hash validator using SHA-256, immutable transaction ledger, admin kill-switch panel, and CSV dataset integration (2000 records)
architectural_notes: Use SHA-256 hashing for Citizen_ID storage and validation
Hash-linked ledger structure: each transaction links to previous hash forming immutable chain
System auto-locks on: ledger tampering, budget exhaustion, hash mismatch, admin pause
Initial budget: ₹10,00,000 INR with real-time deduction tracking
30-day frequency limit per Citizen_ID enforced via Last_Claim_Date comparison
CSV schema: Citizen_ID (12-digit), Income_Tier, Scheme_Eligibility, Scheme_Amount, Last_Claim_Date, Region_Code, Account_Status, Aadhaar_Linked, Claim_Count

existing_features.title: Create admin kill-switch dashboard displaying: System Status Indicator (Active/Paused/Frozen), Emergency Pause Button, Budget Remaining Display, and Transaction Count Monitor. Emergency pause must immediately block all new transactions.

Build transaction processing interface where users can submit benefit claims by entering Citizen_ID and selecting scheme. Display validation results for all three gates, show approval/rejection status with detailed reason, and update transaction history in real-time.

Create beautiful, professional frontend UI for both transaction processing and admin dashboard using modern design patterns. Interface must be intuitive, responsive, and clearly communicate system security features.
Make the generated CSV file accessible as a downloadable static asset at frontend/public/assets/jan_dhan_registry_2000.csv so users can download the dataset directly from the application.

Regenerate the CSV dataset file at frontend/public/assets/jan_dhan_registry_2000.csv with approximately 500 records instead of 2000, maintaining the existing schema with columns: Citizen_ID (12-digit text), Income_Tier, Scheme_Eligibility, Scheme_Amount (number), Last_Claim_Date (DD-MM-YYYY format), Region_Code, Account_Status (Active/Inactive), Aadhaar_Linked (TRUE/FALSE), Claim_Count (number). Preserve data variety including different income tiers, multiple scheme types (Pension, Health, Education), various region codes, mix of Active/Inactive accounts, TRUE/FALSE Aadhaar linkage status, and claim counts ranging from 0 to 5.

Generate a new CSV dataset file at frontend/public/assets/jan_dhan_registry_2000.csv containing approximately 500 employee records with columns: employee_id (text), name (text), email (text), phone (text), department (text), designation (text), date_of_joining (DD-MM-YYYY format), salary (number), location (text), status (Active/Inactive). Include variety across departments (HR, Finance, IT, Operations, Marketing, Sales), multiple designations (Manager, Senior Executive, Executive, Team Lead), various locations (Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, Chennai, Hyderabad, Pune), mix of Active/Inactive status, salaries ranging from 25000 to 150000, and joining dates between 2018 and 2024.

Update transaction processing interface in frontend/src/components/TransactionForm.tsx to work with employee records instead of citizen records. Replace Citizen_ID input with employee_id input, update scheme selection to reflect employee-relevant schemes or benefits (e.g., Salary Advance, Medical Reimbursement, Travel Allowance), and adjust validation logic to check employee eligibility based on department, designation, status, and other employee-specific criteria.

Update transaction history display in frontend/src/components/TransactionHistory.tsx to show employee-specific transaction details including employee_id, employee name, department, designation, benefit type, amount, approval/rejection status, and timestamp.

Update application branding and labels throughout the UI to reflect employee benefit processing instead of citizen benefit processing. Change 'Jan-Dhan Gateway' header to 'Employee Benefits Gateway', update form labels to use employee terminology, and adjust all user-facing text to reflect the employee benefits context.

new_feature_requests.summary: ID-Hash Validator with SHA-256 for Citizen_ID deduplication and replay protection
Immutable hash-linked ledger with timestamp, hash chain, and tamper detection
Three-key verification: eligibility gate (status, Aadhaar, scheme), budget gate (₹10L), frequency gate (30-day block)
Admin kill-switch panel with system status, pause button, budget display, transaction monitor
CSV dataset integration with 2000 records matching jan_dhan_registry_advanced schema
Beautiful frontend UI for transaction processing and admin dashboard

Create backend data model for Jan-Dhan Registry with fields: Citizen_ID (12-digit text), Income_Tier, Scheme_Eligibility, Scheme_Amount (number), Last_Claim_Date (timestamp), Region_Code, Account_Status (Active/Inactive), Aadhaar_Linked (boolean), Claim_Count (number). Support CSV import of approximately 2000 records.
Implement ID-Hash Validator that converts every 12-digit Citizen_ID into SHA-256 hash for processing. Store and compare hashes to detect duplicate claims and block replay attempts immediately.

Build immutable hash-linked ledger that records each approved transaction with: Timestamp, Citizen_ID_Hash, Scheme, Amount, Previous_Line_Hash, Current_Line_Hash. Current_Hash must equal SHA256(Timestamp + CitizenHash + Scheme + Amount + PreviousHash). After every transaction, compute full file integrity hash and freeze system on mismatch.

Implement Gate 1: Eligibility Gate validation that checks Citizen_ID exists in registry, Account_Status equals Active, Aadhaar_Linked equals TRUE, Scheme_Eligibility matches requested scheme, Scheme_Amount matches fixed scheme mapping, and Claim_Count is 3 or less (reject or flag for manual review if exceeded).
Implement Gate 2: Budget Gate with initial budget of ₹10,00,000 INR. Deduct amount only after approval, reject transaction if insufficient balance, and auto-lock system when budget reaches zero.
Implement Gate 3: Frequency Gate that blocks claims within 30 days of Last_Claim_Date. Reject transaction immediately if violation occurs.

Implement automatic system lock mechanism that freezes all new transactions when: ledger tampering is detected, budget is exhausted, hash chain mismatch occurs, or admin manually pauses the system. Blocked transactions must show clear rejection reason.
Generate CSV dataset file with 2000 synthetic records matching jan_dhan_registry_advanced schema and link it to the project

Generate a CSV file containing 2000 synthetic records matching the jan_dhan_registry_advanced schema with columns: Citizen_ID (12-digit text), Income_Tier, Scheme_Eligibility, Scheme_Amount (number), Last_Claim_Date (DD-MM-YYYY format), Region_Code, Account_Status (Active/Inactive), Aadhaar_Linked (TRUE/FALSE), Claim_Count (number). Ensure data variety includes different income tiers, multiple scheme types (Pension, Health, Education), various region codes, mix of Active/Inactive accounts, TRUE/FALSE Aadhaar linkage status, and claim counts ranging from 0 to 5.
Add a visible download link or button in the application UI (either in the admin dashboard or a dedicated section) that allows users to download the jan_dhan_registry_2000.csv dataset file.
Reduce CSV dataset from 2000 records to approximately 500 records while maintaining data variety

Update backend data model in backend/main.mo to support employee registry with fields: employee_id (Text), name (Text), email (Text), phone (Text), department (Text), designation (Text), date_of_joining (Text for DD-MM-YYYY format), salary (Nat), location (Text), status (Text for Active/Inactive). Replace or extend existing CitizenRecord type to accommodate employee data structure.

Update Gate 1: Eligibility Gate validation logic to check that employee_id exists in employee registry, status equals Active, department and designation match eligibility criteria for requested benefit scheme, and salary falls within appropriate range for the benefit type.
